Home Events Strait of Hormuz Closure (April 2026)

Strait of Hormuz Closure (April 2026)

Escalating
Persian Gulf / Strait of Hormuz Energy securitynaval standoffminingFrance's independent regional role

Timeline

2026-04-14

Starmer states UK minesweepers are "focused on getting the Strait full open" rather than enforcing the US blockade

Sources: Al Jazeera (Middle East)

2026-04-14

Starmer announces UK will co-host talks with France this week to address Hormuz crisis; Von der Leyen warns Hormuz closure and Israeli strikes on Lebanon threaten regional stability

Sources: Al Jazeera (Middle East), Al Jazeera (Middle East)

2026-04-14

Trump claims Tehran wants a deal; Vance reports "progress" in Islamabad talks with door remaining open

Sources: Al Jazeera (Middle East), Reuters (Western)

2026-04-14

US enforcing Hormuz blockade with air power and carrier groups

Sources: Middle East Eye (Middle East)

2026-04-14

Lavrov tells Iran conflict has "no military solution" — Russia offers to mediate between US and Iran

Sources: Middle East Eye (Middle East)

2026-04-14

Iran demands compensation from five regional states over war damage; Tehran seeking reparations from states it holds partially responsible for supporting US-Israel war effort

Sources: Middle East Eye (Middle East)

2026-04-14

US blockade operational with carrier groups enforcing it; USS Abraham Lincoln spotted 200 km from Iran — closest US vessel approach since operations began

Sources: TASS (Russia-aligned), Middle East Eye (Middle East), SCMP (China-aligned)

2026-04-14

Iran prepared dozens of oil-loaded tankers as leverage; experts estimate up to 170 million barrels of oil on these tankers

Sources: TASS (Russia-aligned)

2026-04-14

IMF, World Bank, IEA warn of worst global energy disruption ever; IEA Executive Director Birol: over 80 oil and gas facilities across West Asia damaged to date

Sources: The Hindu (India), SCMP (China-aligned)

2026-04-14

Jet fuel reached nearly $198 in Asia; Asia air travel faces jet fuel squeeze

Sources: SCMP (China-aligned)

2026-04-14

Lavrov in Beijing for official talks with Wang Yi; Russia renews offer to receive Iran's enriched uranium

Sources: TASS (Russia-aligned), Middle East Eye (Middle East)

2026-04-14

JD Vance reports "a lot of progress" in Islamabad talks; first US-Iran meeting "at such a high level" in history of current Iranian leadership

Sources: TASS (Russia-aligned), TASS (Russia-aligned)

2026-04-14

Second round of US-Iran talks being considered for Thursday (April 16) in Islamabad; Geneva and Islamabad as potential venues

Sources: TASS (Russia-aligned), TASS (Russia-aligned)

2026-04-14

China March trade data reflects Hormuz disruption; exports rose only 2.5% (missed 4% forecast), imports surged 27.8% as transport costs and energy prices increased

Sources: SCMP (China-aligned)

2026-04-14

Kim Jong Un observes major weapons test of strategic cruise missiles, anti-warship missiles — Korean Central News Agency reports two strategic cruise missiles and three anti-warship missiles fired during test

Sources: TASS (Russia-aligned)

2026-04-14

Canada's Carney secures majority government through special election wins; Liberals won three vacant seats including two Toronto districts, giving Carney's party 171+ seats

Sources: SCMP (China-aligned)

2026-04-14

Pakistan faces economic strain; fails to rollover $3B UAE loan; Finance Minister considering financing options amid soaring oil prices

Sources: SCMP (China-aligned)

2026-04-14

Pakistan has been acting as mediator in US-Iran talks

Sources: SCMP (China-aligned)

2026-04-14

EU top diplomat Kallas: Europe ready to work with others to ensure freedom of navigation but unclear on US approach to Hormuz

Sources: TASS (Russia-aligned)

2026-04-14

NATO allies publicly refuse to join Trump's Iranian port blockade — Allied refusal creates rift within alliance; US proceeds with blockade unilaterally

Sources: Reuters (Western)

2026-04-14

Asia markets surge, oil falls on US-Iran talks optimism — new development since 0500 snapshot; Trump quoted Iranian officials as "keen on a deal"

Sources: Al Jazeera (Middle East)

2026-04-14

US energy secretary Wright expects oil prices to peak in coming weeks amid market optimism on diplomatic progress

Sources: TASS (Russia-aligned)

2026-04-14

Australia signals potential participation in Hormuz Strait freedom of navigation mission; Deputy PM Marles says Australia working with partners to identify possible contributions

Sources: TASS (Russia-aligned)

2026-04-14

China says US blockade of Iran ports "dangerous and irresponsible"; Beijing criticizes US maritime restrictions as escalation; China positioning as alternative diplomatic actor

Sources: France24 (Western), DW (Western)

2026-04-14

Vice-Premier Ding Xuexiang traveling to Turkmenistan for gas field visits; China offsetting shipping disruptions from Iran war

Sources: SCMP (China-aligned)

2026-04-14

Malaysia declares "critical period" for fuel by June as Hormuz blockade accelerates Asian energy crisis; Deputy PM says Malaysia scrambling to mitigate fallout affecting 25% of global seaborne oil trade

Sources: SCMP (China-aligned)

2026-04-14

India fertilizer shortage deepens amid ongoing Iran war; global fertilizer markets disrupted by Hormuz closure; Indian agriculture facing severe input shortages

Sources: NPR (Western)

2026-04-14

US-sanctioned tankers pass Strait of Hormuz despite blockade; Reuters reports enforcement gap as traders sense potential diplomatic breakthrough

Sources: Reuters (Western)

2026-04-14

Indonesia, US sign major defence cooperation agreement; US seeking overflight access in Indonesia for military planes; Jakarta positioning itself between US and other powers

Sources: Al Jazeera (Middle East)

2026-04-14

Most Israelis oppose Iran ceasefire despite war-weariness — poll; tension between public fatigue and demand for sustained pressure on Tehran

Sources: BBC (Western)

2026-04-14

Trump reaffirms naval blockade needed to stop Iran "blackmailing the world"; VP Vance: "Iran chose not to accept our terms" after failed negotiations; blockade continues

Sources: BBC (Western)

2026-04-14

IEA warns of global oil demand destruction as Iran war disrupts energy markets

Sources: Al Jazeera (Regional)

2026-04-14

First sanctioned Chinese tanker Rich Starry becomes first sanctioned vessel to transit Hormuz under US naval observation; signals potential shadow fleet workaround

Sources: Al Jazeera (Regional), Jerusalem Post (Israel)

2026-04-14

Lavrov visits China as Russia coordinates Middle East response with Beijing; both nations vocal critics of US-Israel war on Iran

Sources: Al Jazeera (Regional)

2026-04-14

FAO warns of global food "catastrophe" if Hormuz disruption persists; global agriculture highly exposed; higher commodity prices and food inflation expected

Sources: Al Jazeera (Regional), SCMP (China-aligned)

2026-04-14

France, UK to co-host virtual Hormuz shipping security talks amid blockade stalemate; Western allies seeking to coordinate response to keep Strait open without endorsing US blockade

Sources: The Hindu (India), Al Jazeera (Regional)

2026-04-14

Italy halts defense agreement renewal with Israel, blocks Sicily base use for Israeli forces; first European country to formally suspend defense ties with Israel over the Iran war

Sources: Middle East Eye (Regional), Jerusalem Post (Israel)

2026-04-14

Asia's energy supply at breaking point as Hormuz crisis deepens; peace talks stalled with no clear timeline for oil flows to resume

Sources: SCMP (China-aligned)

2026-04-14

US-Iran negotiating teams could return to Islamabad this week — report; Trump claims Tehran wants a deal while Iranian officials deny US "goodwill"

Sources: Jerusalem Post (Israel)

2026-04-14

Iran delegation claims attack en route to Islamabad; regime re-arming — report

Sources: Jerusalem Post (Israel)

2026-04-14

Russia, UAE call for immediate Gulf ceasefire as Qatar shifts tone; Qatar said focus should be on ending fighting, not debating Hormuz status

Sources: Middle East Eye (Regional)

2026-04-14

Saudi Arabia seeks Pakistani "nuclear umbrella"; 10-18 combat aircraft reportedly deployed to Saudi Arabia; claim could signal nuclear escalation signaling

Sources: TASS (Russia-aligned)

2026-04-14

China dismisses Vice Minister of Foreign Affairs Sun Weidong; sudden dismissal part of broader anticorruption campaign; comes amid intense diplomatic activity on Iran

Sources: Al Jazeera (Regional)

2026-04-14

IDF surrounds Bint Jbeil in Lebanon, 10 soldiers wounded in Hezbollah clashes; three soldiers in serious condition; operation continues as Israel-Lebanon Washington talks proceed

Sources: Jerusalem Post (Israel)

2026-04-14

Druzhba pipeline repair by end of April — Zelensky; pipeline damaged in conflict would restore partial oil flow to Europe

Sources: TASS (Russia-aligned)

2026-04-14

Germany, Ukraine upgrade relations to strategic partnership; Friedrich Merz confirmed Germany will continue supporting Ukraine's EU accession path

Sources: TASS (Russia-aligned)

2026-04-14

Internet blackout in Iran lasts over 45 days; over 1,080 hours of connectivity disruption reported

Sources: TASS (Russia-aligned)

2026-04-14

Hungary's next PM would call Putin and urge Ukraine ceasefire; Péter Magyar would pick up if Putin calls; Hungary maintains pragmatic Russia relations

Sources: BBC (Western)

2026-04-14

Two ships from Iran pass Hormuz despite US blockade; ICRC delivers first batch of emergency relief to Iran; Iran plans to "neutralize marine blockade threat through border trade"

Sources: France24 (Western), Al Jazeera (Regional), TASS (Russia-aligned)

2026-04-14

IMF cuts global growth forecast to 2.8%; IEA, World Bank heads warn of Iran war impacts; FAO warns of global food "catastrophe" if Hormuz disruption persists; UN warns 8 million in Asia-Pacific risk poverty

Sources: TASS (Russia-aligned), SCMP (China-aligned), Middle East Eye (Regional)

2026-04-14

New Mossad chief Barnea: Iran mission incomplete until regime change; IRGC recruiting 12-year-olds; analysis reveals Iran forces at "highest level of readiness"

Sources: Jpost (Regional), Jpost (Regional)

2026-04-14

German lawyers file criminal complaint against Merz for "aiding genocide"; Smotrich refers to Nazis in branding Germany "hypocrites"

Sources: Jpost (Regional)

2026-04-14

Russia introduces helium export restrictions; 217,000 cyberattacks thwarted in Russia in 2025

Sources: Moscow Times (Russia-aligned), TASS (Russia-aligned)

2026-04-14

Xi holds bilateral meetings: Spain PM Sanchez, Vietnam leader To Lam in Beijing; Xi calls on Spain to jointly resist "law of the jungle"; Vietnam's To Lam fast-tracks Beijing visit

Sources: CGTN (China-aligned), CGTN (China-aligned), SCMP (China-aligned)

2026-04-14

UK finance minister criticizes Washington over Hormuz disruption; UK's finance minister publicly criticizing US approach

Sources: Al Jazeera (Regional), Reuters (Western)

2026-04-14

UN warns Iran war causing fertiliser shortages for developing countries; food security crisis spreading beyond Middle East

Sources: Middle East Eye (Regional)

2026-04-14

Asia's energy supply at breaking point as Hormuz blockade continues; Malaysia, Indonesia, Nepal all reporting economic strain; UN warns 8 million Asia-Pacific residents could fall into poverty if conflict continues

Sources: SCMP (China-aligned)

2026-04-14

IMF cuts global growth forecast to 3.1%, revises World Economic Outlook down 0.2 percentage points; China GDP forecast cut to 4.4%; IMF warns of global recession risk

Sources: SCMP (China-aligned), Middle East Eye (Regional)

2026-04-13

Pentagon spokesperson confirmed vessels traveling to/from non-Iranian ports will be permitted passage through Strait of Hormuz — US Department of War will begin enforcing blockade April 13 at 2:00 PM GMT.

Sources: TASS (Russia-aligned), BBC (Western), SCMP (China-aligned)

2026-04-13

RDIF CEO warns oil price may exceed $150/barrel this week amid Hormuz blockade disruption

Sources: TASS (Russia-aligned)

2026-04-13

UK and France planning separate naval mission to Strait of Hormuz to "protect freedom of navigation" — marking direct divergence from US enforcement action

Sources: Middle East Eye (Middle East), 11:36 GMT

2026-04-13

Switzerland offers mediation between US and Iran over Hormuz blockade

Sources: TASS (Russia-aligned)

2026-04-13

China warns against sending weapons to Iran amid escalating tensions

Sources: SCMP (China-aligned)

2026-04-13

US naval blockade of Iranian ports takes effect 14:00 GMT April 13; vessels to/from non-Iranian ports may pass; IRGC views military vessels approaching strait as ceasefire violations

Sources: BBC (Western), Al Jazeera (Middle East), TASS (Russia-aligned), Middle East Eye (Middle East)

2026-04-13

Oil above $103/barrel; Asian markets continue to feel impact as naval blockade disrupts Hormuz transit. ADNOC CEO stated "Strait of Hormuz not Iran's to control." Two supertankers made U-turns April 12.

Sources: Multiple feeds

2026-04-13

Malaysia declares "critical period" for fuel by June as Hormuz blockade accelerates Asian energy crisis; Singapore and Australia vow uninterrupted supply

Sources: SCMP (China-aligned), TASS (Russia-aligned)

2026-04-13

ADB warned of "formidable test" for Asian economies amid oil price shock; Philippines faces LPG price shock affecting beef stew staple prices

Sources: The Hindu (India)

2026-04-13

Iran says will charge ships from hostile nations tolls to cross Hormuz; navy confirms full control over Strait despite US blockade

Sources: TASS (Russia-aligned), RT (Russia-aligned), Al Jazeera (Middle East), Middle East Eye (Middle East)

2026-04-13

Iran denies charging tolls for Indian tankers specifically (contradicting earlier reports)

Sources: Middle East Eye (Middle East)

2026-04-13

Spain's defense minister called NATO decision to refuse blockade "zero sense."

Sources: Middle East Eye (Middle East)

2026-04-13

Physical oil hits record high near $150/barrel as Hormuz crisis deepens

Sources: Reuters (Western)

2026-04-13

Von der Leyen calls restoring Hormuz traffic of "paramount" importance — strongest EU-level language yet; signals growing impatience with open-ended blockade

Sources: Reuters (Western)

2026-04-13

India receives first Iranian oil shipments in seven years amid blockade chaos

Sources: Reuters (Western)

2026-04-13

OPEC lowers second-quarter global oil demand forecast due to Iran war

Sources: Reuters (Western)

2026-04-13

US-Iran peace talks stall amid continued blockade tensions

Sources: Reuters (Western)

2026-04-13

Russia warns Middle East conflict could increase number of people facing hunger worldwide by 45 million to a record 673 million

Sources: TASS (Russia-aligned)

2026-04-13

EU expenses for energy imports have grown by 22 billion euros since the Middle East conflict began

Sources: TASS (Russia-aligned)

2026-04-13

Trump says Iran wants to make a deal — potential diplomatic off-ramp; contradicts earlier administration statements

Sources: Reuters (Western)

2026-04-13

Japan risks summer power crunch due to Middle East LNG disruptions

Sources: Reuters (Western)

2026-04-13

NATO allies publicly refuse to join Trump's Iranian port blockade — first explicit NATO-wide confirmation of refusal; significant fracture signal

Sources: Reuters (Western)

2026-04-13

Iran war weighs on global economy as IMF meeting starts

Sources: Reuters (Western)

2026-04-13

Chevron, Shell to sign agreements for oil, gas areas in Venezuela

Sources: Reuters (Western)

2026-04-13

Turkey says NATO should reset ties with Trump at next summit

Sources: Reuters (Western)

2026-04-13

US Navy deploying underwater drones to clear Iranian mines from Strait of Hormuz — first US confirmation of active mine-clearance operation; confirms mines present and constitute operational challenge

Sources: Defense Scoop via Hacker News (keyword: nuclear)

2026-04-13

Vance says US-Iran Peace Talks Break Down over Nuclear Issue — diplomatic off-ramp closing; nuclear demands remain sticking point

Sources: WSJ via Hacker News (29 pts, 8 comments)

2026-04-13

World Bank chief sounds alarm about looming jobs crisis even after war ends

Sources: Reuters (Western)

2026-04-13

Trump says gas prices may remain high through November midterm election — first explicit link between Hormuz crisis and US electoral calendar

Sources: Reuters (Western)

2026-04-13

Rheinmetall and Destinus team up in new missile venture — European defense industrial cooperation deepens amid crisis

Sources: Reuters (Western)

2026-04-13

US, Australia, Philippines hold second joint drills in South China Sea this year

Sources: Reuters (Western)

2026-04-13

US naval blockade of Iran's ports now active — headline changed from "Deadline passes" to "US begins blockade"; confirms operational commencement

Sources: Reuters (Western)

2026-04-13

Hungary's Magyar does not back Ukraine's fast-track EU entry — EU unity on Iran response further complicated

Sources: Reuters (Western)

2026-04-13

Trump threatens to "sink" Iranian boats that challenge Hormuz blockade; direct escalation of rhetoric

Sources: The Hindu (India), BBC (Western)

2026-04-13

UK explicitly breaks ranks, stating it will not support US naval blockade of Strait of Hormuz

Sources: BBC (Western)

2026-04-13

Iran reports dozens of oil-loaded tankers positioned near Strait of Hormuz; experts estimate up to 170 million barrels of oil may be on these tankers

Sources: TASS (Russia-aligned), Al Jazeera (Middle East)

2026-04-13

Next round of US-Iran talks reportedly set for Islamabad on Thursday (April 16)

Sources: TASS (Russia-aligned), Al Jazeera (Middle East)

2026-04-13

Lavrov reiterates Russia's readiness to contribute to resolving Hormuz crisis

Sources: TASS (Russia-aligned)

2026-04-12

Iran: no change until common framework reached — Iran says Strait status unchanged until both sides agree on a framework with US

Sources: TASS (Russia-aligned), CNN, 05:33

2026-04-12

IRGC: will prevent all military vessels from crossing — Revolutionary Guard commander said Iran would block all military ships from transiting

Source: TASS (Russia-aligned), 00:58

2026-04-12

US warships transited in mine clearance operation: Pentagon said two US ships crossed in a demining mission; Iran denied the transit occurred without coordination

Sources: India (India), TASS (Russia-aligned), 08:26

2026-04-12

Three oil supertankers exited: Three supertankers laden with oil exited the Strait — first commercial transits since the ceasefire

Sources: Al Jazeera (Middle East), 05:00

2026-04-12

UN maritime chief: Iran must not charge tolls

Source: Al Jazeera (Middle East), 03:20

2026-04-12

Two supertankers U-turned in Strait of Hormuz: Bloomberg reports three large crude carriers approached from Gulf of Oman late Saturday, at least two U-turned — suggesting continued commercial shipping uncertainty

Sources: TASS (Russia-aligned), 12:26

2026-04-12

Trump announces naval blockade of Strait of Hormuz — blocking all ships entering or leaving waterway — major military escalation directly threatening Iranian oil exports

Source: brief-143000, 14:30 UTC

2026-04-12

Trump announces sweeping naval blockade of Strait of Hormuz after 21-hour Pakistan talks end without agreement

Sources: Reuters (Western), AP News (Western), SCMP (China-aligned), TASS (Russia-aligned), Al Jazeera (Middle East)

2026-04-12

IRGC threatens to trap US forces in "deadly vortex" in the strait; says it will prevent ALL military vessels from crossing

Sources: Middle East Eye, The Hindu

2026-04-12

Iran denies US warships transited the strait; US claims mine-clearing operation underway

Sources: Multiple feeds

2026-04-12

Oil prices spiking; RDIF CEO Dmitriev projects $150/barrel achievable this week

Sources: TASS (Russia-aligned)

2026-04-12

EU airline industry warns of fuel shortages if Hormuz remains closed

Sources: BBC (Western)

2026-04-12

Trump warns China against arming Iran with "staggering" tariffs

Sources: Middle East Eye, SCMP, TASS

2026-04-12

Pope Leo condemns "delusion of omnipotence" driving US-Israel war on Iran

Sources: SCMP (China-aligned)

2026-04-12

Putin offers to mediate US-Iran peace efforts

Sources: Middle East Eye

2026-04-12

CENTCOM deploying underwater drones to clear naval mines in Hormuz; IRGC reiterates "deadly vortex" threat, warns ALL military vessels will be prevented from crossing

Sources: Middle East Eye (Middle East), Jerusalem Post (Israel), BBC (Western)

2026-04-12

Trump confirms US imposing "complete" naval blockade on Iran after Islamabad talks collapse; Iran warns military vessels would breach any ceasefire

Sources: AP (Western), TASS (Russia-aligned), Al Jazeera (Middle East), Middle East Eye (Middle East)

2026-04-12

RDIF CEO warns oil prices may exceed $150/barrel within days as Hormuz blockade disrupts global supply chains

Sources: TASS (Russia-aligned)

2026-04-12

UK publicly rules out participation in naval operations

Sources: Multiple feeds

2026-04-11

Trump: "We're going to open up the gulf with or without them... I think it's going to go pretty quickly"

Sources: Reuters (Western), BBC (Western)

2026-04-11

French facing critical fuel shortages

Sources: RT (Russia-aligned)

2026-04-11

Egypt imposes business curfew to counter soaring fuel costs

Sources: CGTN (China-aligned)

2026-04-11

Asian nations ramp up coal power amid energy crunch

Sources: SCMP (China-aligned)

2026-04-11

Nigerian gasoline pump prices rise by 65%

Sources: Reuters (Western)

2026-04-11

EU slams Hormuz toll idea; Iraq/Saudi Arabia/Israel/Jordan/Gulf states

Sources: The Hindu (India)

2026-04-11

Cathay Pacific cuts ~2% of flights between mid-May and June due to surging jet fuel prices

Source: SCMP (China-aligned)

2026-04-11

ACI Europe updates jet fuel warning with more specific attribution; article substantially revised

Source: BBC (Western)

2026-04-11

SCMP: "Asia's panicked farmers brace for looming rice crisis post-Iran war" — fuel and fertiliser costs hitting Southeast Asian rice production

Source: SCMP (China-aligned)

2026-04-11

SCMP: "'Cancelling orders' in China: how Hormuz oil crisis is hitting transport, manufacturing"

Source: SCMP (China-aligned)

2026-04-11

IEA predicts diesel fuel shortage in Europe by May; estimate called "too optimistic" by Russian official

Source: TASS (Russia-aligned)

2026-04-11

TASS: Italian vessel stranded in Hormuz for over one month

Source: TASS (Russia-aligned)

2026-04-11

Asian nations press US to extend Russian oil sanctions relief

Source: Bloomberg via TASS (Russia-aligned)

2026-04-11

Slovakia demands Druzhba pipeline resumption; EU Commission cannot get Zelensky's response

Source: TASS (Russia-aligned)

2026-04-11

Russian LNG deliveries to Spain rise 120% in March

Source: TASS (Russia-aligned)

2026-04-11

SCMP: Japan's Middle East oil habit gets Iran war reality check — 95% crude from Middle East

Sources: SCMP (China-aligned)

2026-04-11

Trump claims US clearing mines in Hormuz; Iran denies US ships passed through

Sources: TASS (Russia-aligned), RT (Russia-aligned)

2026-04-11

US Navy confirmed transit of Strait of Hormuz without Iranian permission

Sources: Multiple feeds (Western, Middle Eastern)

2026-04-11

US forces actively clearing floated mines — suggests deliberate provocation operation or pre-planned show of force

Sources: Multiple feeds (Western, Middle Eastern)

2026-04-11

Trump stated strait "will reopen" — implying it had been closed or threatened

Sources: Multiple feeds (Western, Middle Eastern)

2026-04-11

Hormuz situation reported as central leverage in ongoing US-Iran talks in Islamabad

Sources: Multiple feeds (Western, Middle Eastern)

2026-04-11

Trump posted US forces "clearing" Strait of Hormuz, with empty tankers heading to US ports to load oil/gas

Source: Truth Social

2026-04-11

Iran strongly rejected Washington's claims that US destroyers entered the strait, with military spokesperson calling it "谎言" (lie)

Source: Iranian military spokesperson via WION

2026-04-11

WION reported Iranian strike footage of massive blast at Qatar's Ras Laffan gas hub — first visible confirmation of Iranian strike damage to Gulf gas infrastructure

Source: WION (India)

2026-04-11

US Central Command stated two guided-missile destroyers transited the strait for mine-clearing operations

Source: US Central Command via Multiple feeds

2026-04-11

IRGC warned military vessels attempting to cross Hormuz without coordination will face "strong response"

Source: IRGC via Multiple feeds

2026-04-11

Iranian military "strongly denied" the US claim

Source: Iranian military via Multiple feeds

2026-04-11

China planning to deliver air defense systems to Iran in coming weeks (per US intelligence)

Source: US intelligence via Multiple feeds

2026-04-11

Reuters: Iran war shock to flip oil market to deficit in 2026, analysts say

Sources: Reuters (Western)

2026-04-11

Reuters: US likely to extend Russian oil waiver to temper Iran war shock

Sources: Reuters (Western)

2026-04-11

SCMP: Singapore-Australia vow uninterrupted fuel supply amid global energy shock

Sources: SCMP (China-aligned)

2026-04-11

SCMP: Iran's Hormuz toll threat lays bare Asia's energy vulnerability

Sources: SCMP (China-aligned)

2026-04-11

Malaysia's Anwar on Singapore's Hormuz stance: "that's their affair" — independent foreign policy

Sources: SCMP (China-aligned)

2026-04-11

Reuters: US consumer sentiment dives to record low in April amid Iran war

Sources: Reuters (Western)

2026-04-11

Reuters: Indian auto hub hikes minimum wage after protests over soaring costs due to Iran war

Sources: Reuters (Western)

2026-04-11

Reuters: Fed's Daly says oil shock means getting inflation down takes longer

Sources: Reuters (Western)

2026-04-11

France accelerates shift to electric power amid global energy price surges

Sources: MEE (Middle East)

2026-04-11

"Iran war leaves crisis-scarred countries counting the cost"

Sources: Reuters (Western)

2026-04-10

Preparations underway for oil export resumption; Hormuz navigation issues persist

Sources: Reuters, BBC, SCMP

2026-04-10

India finalising oil/gas supply to Mauritius amid crisis

Sources: Reuters, BBC, SCMP

2026-04-10

Saudi Arabia cuts oil output after Houthi attacks on East-West Pipeline

Sources: Reuters, SCMP

2026-04-10

EU airline industry warns of jet fuel shortages in three weeks without Hormuz resolution

Sources: Reuters, SCMP

2026-04-10

Singapore, Australia pledge uninterrupted fuel supply amid global energy shock

Sources: Reuters, SCMP

2026-04-10

IEA predicts diesel shortage in Europe by May

Sources: Reuters, SCMP

2026-04-10

Starmer and Trump discussed "military capabilities" to reopen Strait of Hormuz; UK to convene more talks on Hormuz next week

Sources: Al Jazeera, BBC Europe

2026-04-10

EU airline industry warned of jet fuel shortages within 3 weeks if Hormuz remains closed; IEA head Fatah Birol: diesel shortage possible by May; only two EU countries have 90-day jet fuel reserves

Sources: TASS, BBC

2026-04-10

US inflation rises amid Hormuz blockade; gasoline +21.2% in March

Sources: Al Jazeera

2026-04-10

France deploys drone minehunters targeting suspected naval mines in the Strait of Hormuz — acting independently of NATO. Iran affirmed large parts of the passage contain mines that will explode on passing ships.

Sources: France24 (Western)

2026-04-10

Approximately 3,200 vessels remain stranded awaiting passage

Sources: France24, Reuters

2026-04-10

Saudi and UAE military activity in the Gulf increased

Sources: Reuters

2026-04-10

Iran-linked ships driving limited traffic through Hormuz; experts warn food production damage "already done"

Sources: Al Jazeera, Jerusalem Post

2026-04-10

Airline pilots fear retribution over refusing to fly in Middle East

Sources: Jerusalem Post

2026-04-10

Shipping through Strait of Hormuz remains at a trickle despite US-Iran ceasefire; Washington and Tehran accuse each other of not honouring truce agreement.

Sources: Al Jazeera (Middle East)

2026-04-10

~3,200 vessels awaiting passage through Hormuz; Iran vows strait will reopen "after war ends"; Iran stated the passage will reopen "after the war ends."

Sources: Al Jazeera (Middle East), TASS (Russia-aligned), Reuters (Western), France24 (Western)

2026-04-09

Iran has re-closed the Strait of Hormuz, limiting passage to 15 vessels per day under IRGC supervision. Iran's "Hormuz protocol" mandates IRGC supervision and taxes on passage; ADNOC reports 230 loaded tankers awaiting passage.

Sources: Reuters (Western), SCMP (China-aligned), TASS (Russia-aligned)

2026-04-09

25+ India-bound ships stranded due to Hormuz restrictions; Pakistan PM forms crisis team.

Sources: The Hindu (India), SCMP (China-aligned)

2026-04-09

Iran announces alternative routes to bypass Hormuz.

Sources: Reuters (Western), SCMP (China-aligned), Al Jazeera (Middle East), The Hindu (India)

Narrative Divergence

Western sources frame blockade as legitimate enforcement of sanctions and ceasefire terms; Russia-aligned sources emphasize global energy crisis and NATO internal divisions; China-aligned sources note Beijing's dilemma ahead of Xi summit and that open Hormuz serves common interests. UK PM Starmer explicitly refused to support blockade; France, Spain and Germany also opposed or not consulted. UK and France planning separate "humanitarian navigation" mission.

Related Entities

IranFranceSaudi ArabiaUnited Arab EmiratesUnited KingdomUnited StatesEuropean UnionSingaporeAustraliaIndiaJapanChinaSlovakiaSwitzerlandPakistan

Related Markets

No related markets
SI
Signal Intelligence Wiki
Methodology | Last updated: 2026-04-15 15:00 UTC
